headline: Banks Offload $1 Trillion In Loan Risk As SRT Market Surges
summary: Banks are opening a new balance-sheet escape valve, and investors are moving in fast.  Significant risk transfers, or SRTs, have become one of the fastest-growing corners of credit markets, allowing lenders to shift credit risk from loan portfolios to outside investors while reducing the regulatory capital they need to hold.  Banks had offloaded risk tied to more than 905 billion, or $1 trillion, of loans by the end of last year, up 26% from a year earlier, according to the International Association of Credit Portfolio Managers.

raw.headline: Banks Offload $1 Trillion In Loan Risk As SRT Market Surges
raw.summary: Banks are opening a new balance-sheet escape valve, and investors are moving in fast.  Significant risk transfers, or SRTs, have become one of the fastest-growing corners of credit markets, allowing lenders to shift credit risk from loan portfolios to outside investors while reducing the regulatory capital they need to hold.  Banks had offloaded risk tied to more than 905 billion, or $1 trillion, of loans by the end of last year, up 26% from a year earlier, according to the International Association of Credit Portfolio Managers.

headline: Blackstone’s Liftoff IPO And Nippon Life Deal Reshape BX Outlook
summary: Liftoff Mobile, backed by Blackstone (NYSE:BX), has completed an IPO during a busy summer period for new listings. Blackstone has also entered an investment partnership with Nippon Life Insurance focused on private credit and real estate capital deployment. These developments add fresh angles for NYSE:BX beyond recent attention on data centers, fundraising activity, and AI related projects. headline: Blackstone-backed Liftoff Mobile raises $437 million in US IPO
summary: Blackstone-backed Liftoff Mobile said on Wednesday it has raised $437 million in its U.S. initial public offering, as ‌a busy summer for listings gathers pace and encourages ‌more companies to test investor appetite.  The Redwood City, California-based company sold 19 million shares ​and priced its offering at $23 per share, above targeted range of $20 to $22 apiece.  The IPO valued it at $3.83 billion, according to outstanding shares disclosed in its IPO prospectus.

The IPO valued it at $3.83 billion, according to outstanding shares disclosed in its IPO prospectus.
